questions.question_text: ________ was the little kitty's name.
questions.explanation_text: Mahendran was the little Kitty's name.

questions.question_text: Meena shares with Mridu

options.label: A
options.text: the biryani cooked by Rukku Mani

options.label: B
options.text: the secret about the cat in the backyard

options.label: D
options.text: the advice given by the beggar that

questions.correct_answer: the secret about the cat in the backyard
questions.explanation_text: the secret about the cat in the backyard

questions.question_text: On seeing the chappals, the music-master

options.label: C
options.text: picked them to keep in his bag

questions.question_text: Which of these is true about Ravi?

options.label: C
options.text: He has the habit of stealing food.

options.label: D
options.text: He likes to show off his knowledge.

questions.correct_answer: He likes to show off his knowledge.
questions.explanation_text: He likes to show off his knowledge.

questions.question_text: What was kept out for drying in the backyard of Rukku Manni's home? (A pair of slippers)
questions.explanation_text: A bamboo tray full of red chillies had been set out to dry in the backyard of Rukku Manni's house. The kitten accidentally tripped a few chillies over it while trying to hide beneath the tray.

questions.question_text: The music-master's reaction to the new pair of chappals shows his
With whom did the music teacher compare Ravi in the plot, A gift of chappals?
questions.explanation_text: The music teacher compared Ravi with Lord Hanuman and called him an incarnate of the Hindu God. The teacher actually wanted to call him a monkey but chose to say it politely by addressing him as A Hanuman incarnate.

questions.question_text: What were Mridu's cousins eager to show her?
questions.explanation_text: The children found a very small kitten outside their house's gate on the morning of the same day when Mridu came to visit them. Mridu's cousins were very excited to show her the newly found kitten whom they had hidden in their backyard.

questions.question_text: Where was the newly found kitten kept by Mridu's cousins?
questions.explanation_text: Mridu's cousins kept the newly found kitten inside a torn football that was lined with sacking and was filled with sand.

questions.question_text: The pair of chappals was ________.

options.label: D
options.text: both torn and covered with dust

questions.explanation_text: The pair of chappals were grey covered with dust.

questions.question_text: To whom were the music master's slippers gifted by Mridu and her cousins?
questions.explanation_text: The innocent children pitied the beggar and gifted him the music master's odd-looking chappals. The beggar was barefoot and had blisters all under his feet, so, Mridu and her cousins gifted him the music master's chappals as they were a perfect fit for him.

questions.question_text: Where did Ravi hide the kitten?

options.label: B
options.text: Behind a thick bitter berry bush

questions.explanation_text: The kitten was hidden in the backyard, behind a thick bitter-berry bush, inside a torn football lined with sacking and filled with sand.

questions.question_text: Rukku Manni told Ravi to tell the beggar
How did Rukku Manni feel when Lalli told her about the missing chappals ​​​​​​?
questions.explanation_text: Rukku Manni felt harassed for she was worried and upset to think that someone would have stolen the slippers from their house and all that was quite embarrassing.

questions.question_text: Why did Rukku Manni most likely ask the beggar to leave?

options.label: A
options.text: He was eating all their food.

options.label: D
options.text: He was making a habit of visiting them.

questions.correct_answer: He was making a habit of visiting them.
questions.explanation_text: He was making a habit of visiting them.

questions.question_text: What makes Mridu conclude that the beggar has no money to buy chappals?
What is the secret that Meena shares with Mridu in the backyard?
Walking towards the kitchen with Mridu and Meena, Rukku Manni began to laugh. What made her laugh?
The music master is making lovely music. Read aloud the sentence in the text that expresses this idea. (Answer the context of A Gift of Chappals)
Who learned music from the music master? What instrument did she play?
Mridu had noticed in front of Meena's house a pair of chappals. Whom did they belong to?
Whose chappals were given to the music master? Were they old or new?
Who does he say the kitten's ancestors are? Do you believe him? [A Gift of Chappals]
How did the beggar win the sympathy of others in the story A Gift of Chappals?
The beggar was leaning against what in Ravi's garden? (A Gift of Chappals)
Why were the red chilli kept in the backyard? (A Gift of Chappals)
Why were Meena's eyes full of tears?
What does she suggest to show her concern? Answer in the content of A Gift of Chappals.
Describe the music teacher, as seen from the window. [A Gift of Chappals]
How does Ravi get milk for the kitten?
On getting Gopu Mama's chappals, the music teacher tried not to look too happy. Why?
On getting a gift of chappals, the beggar vanished in a minute. Why was he in such a hurry to leave?
What was the noise that startled Mridu and frightened Mahendran?
Describe the way Lalli was playing the violin.
Describe the beggar's condition according to story A Gift of Chappals.

Fill in the blanks in the following paragraph:
Today is Sunday. I'm wondering whether I should stay at home or go out. If I ________ (go) out, I ________ (miss) the lovely Sunday lunch at home. If I ________ (stay) for lunch, I ________ (miss) the Sunday film showing at Archana Theatre. I think I'll go out and see the film, only to avoid getting too fat.

Complete each sentence below by appropriately using any one of the following:
if you want to/if you don't want to/if you want him to
Don't go to the theatre _________
He'll post your letter _________
Please use my pen ________
He'll lend you his umbrella ________
My neighbour, Ramesh, will take you to the doctor ________
Don't eat it ________
Describe Ravi's character in the story A Gift of Chappals.
Had the beggar come to Rukku Manni's house for the first time? Give reasons for your answer.
Was Rukku Manni right when she took the side of the children?
Why did the cat's name Mahendran pleased Mridu?

Complete the following sentences:
Ravi compares Lalli's playing the violin to ________.
Trying to hide beneath the tray of chillies, Mahendran ________.
The teacher played a few notes on his violin and Lalli ________.
The beggar said that the kind ladies of the household ________.
After the lesson was over, the music teacher asked Lalli if ________.
A sharp V-shaped line had formed between her eyebrows. What does it suggest to you about Rukku Manni's mood?
How did the cat react when he heard the sound made by Ravi?

Read the following extract and answer the questions that follow:
"I'll go, I'll go!" he said wearily. "Only let me have a rest here under this tree. The sun is so hot, the tar has melted on the road. My feet are already blistered." He stretched out his feet to show large, pink peeling blisters on the soles of his bare feet.
"I suppose he doesn't have the money to buy chappals," Mridu whispered to Meena-Ravi. "Have you got an old pair in the house somewhere?"
I'll go, I'll go!

Who said this?
When did he say this?


Why were the beggar's feet blistered?
I suppose he doesn't have the money to buy chappals.

Who said this to whom?
Who is he here?


What did Mridu suggest?

Read the passage carefully and answer the following questions that follow each of them:(1x5=5)
"Ravi, tell him there's nothing left in the kitchen!" called Rukku Manni. "And he's not to come again-tell him that!" She sounded fed up. Ravi didn't have to repeat it all to the beggar. What his mother said had been easy for them all to hear, there under the neem tree. The beggar sat up and sighed. "I'll go,!" he said wearily. " Only let me have a rest here under this tree. The sun is so hot, the tar has melted on the road. My feet are already blistered." He stretched out his feet to show large, pink, peeling blisters on the soles of his bare feet.
What did Rukku Manni instruct to Ravi?
How did her words affect the beggar?
Why did his feet have blisters?
How did Ravi help him?
Pick out the word from the passage that means: in a tired manner.

Read the following extract and answer the questions that follow:
The music-master came out of the house and took an unappreciative look at the three of them sitting quietly under the tree, playing marbles. Then he searched for his chappals in the verandah, where he had put them. "Lalli!" he called, after a few moments. She hurried up to him. "Have you seen my chappals, my dear? I remember having kept them here!" Ravi, Mridu and Meena silently watched Lalli and the music-master search every corner of the verandah. He scurried around, looking over the railing and crouching near the flower pots to look between them. "Brand new, they were! I went all the way to Mount Road to buy them!" he went on saying. "They cost a whole month's fees, do you know?"
Whom was the music-master teaching?
Why do you think the music-master took an unappreciative look at the three children?
How did the children react when they saw music teacher and Lalli looking for the chappals?
Give two reasons that the music master didn't want to lose his chappals.

Read the passage and answer the following questions:
Ravi, tell him there's nothing left in the kitchen. Called Rukku Manni. "And he's not to come again — tell him that!" She sounded fed up.
Ravi didn't have to repeat it all to the beggar. What his mother said had been easy for all of them to hear, there under the neem tree, the beggar sat up and sighed.
"I'll go, I'll go." he said wearily, "only let me have a rest here under this tree. The sun is so hot, the tar has melted on the road. My feet are already blistered." He stretched out his feet to show large, pink peeling blisters on the soles of his bare feet.
Who was given instructions to send away the beggar?
How did the beggar react to her words?
Why did his feet have blisters?
Pick out the word from the passage that means the same to 'to do again'.

Read the following extract and answer the questions that follow:
Ssh! said Ravi. "I'm thinking about it! Blubbering, 'it's not fair, it's not fair' isn't going to help. In two minutes he'll be frying his feet on that road. What he needs is a pair of chappals. So where do we get them? Come, let's search the house." He pushed Mridu and Meena into the house.
Just as she stepped into the verandah, Mridu's eyes fell on the odd-looking chappals she had noticed when she arrived. "Ravi!" she whispered to him. "Whose are those?"
I'm thinking about it!"

Who said this to whom?
What was the speaker thinking about?


Where did Mridu find the chappals?
Whose chappals were they?
Whom did the children want to give them? What does this tell about them?

Read the passage and answer the questions:
We found him outside the gate this morning. He was meowing and meowing, poor thing, said Meena. "It's a secret, Amma says Paati will leave for our Paddu Mama's house if she knows we have a cat."
Whom does 'we' and 'he' refer to in these lines?
Where did they find him?
Why was his presence kept as a secret?
Pick out a word from the above passage which is opposite to 'inside'.
